metadata.description: Standardized examination catalog. Doctors must select examination names from this list when ordering tests.

examinations.name: Abdominal Examination
examinations.description: Palpation, percussion, and auscultation to assess abdominal organs and tenderness.

examinations.name: Abdominal Thrust Maneuver
examinations.description: Upward abdominal thrusts to relieve foreign-body airway obstruction.

examinations.name: Anterior Drawer Test
examinations.description: Assesses anterior cruciate ligament laxity by anterior tibial translation.

examinations.name: Anterior Rhinoscopy
examinations.description: Visual inspection of nasal cavity using speculum and light source.

examinations.name: Anthropometric Measurements
examinations.description: Measures body size and proportions, such as height, weight, and circumferences.

examinations.name: Apgar Score
examinations.description: Rapid newborn assessment of heart rate, breathing, tone, reflexes, and color.

examinations.name: Basset's Sign
examinations.description: Elicits pain by compressing the femoral head in suspected hip pathology.

examinations.name: Brachial Plexus Tension Test
examinations.description: Provokes symptoms by stretching brachial plexus to assess nerve involvement.

examinations.name: Breast Examination
examinations.description: Inspects and palpates breasts and lymph nodes for masses or abnormalities.

examinations.name: Capillary Fragility Test (Tourniquet Test)
examinations.description: Assesses capillary fragility by counting petechiae after cuff occlusion.

examinations.name: Chest Expansion Measurement
examinations.description: Measures thoracic excursion during inspiration and expiration.

examinations.name: Comprehensive Eye Examination
examinations.description: Full assessment of vision, eye structures, and ocular function.

examinations.name: Corneal Examination
examinations.description: Evaluates corneal clarity and defects, often with fluorescein staining.

examinations.name: Cover Test
examinations.description: Detects ocular misalignment by observing refixation during occlusion.

examinations.name: Dermoscopy
examinations.description: Magnified skin lesion evaluation to improve diagnosis of pigmented lesions.

examinations.name: Digital Rectal Examination (DRE)
examinations.description: Palpates rectum and prostate to assess masses, tone, or tenderness.

examinations.name: Eversion Stress Test
examinations.description: Assesses lateral ankle ligament injury by stressing the joint into eversion.

examinations.name: Exophthalmometry
examinations.description: Measures degree of eye protrusion to assess proptosis.

examinations.name: External Eye Examination
examinations.description: Inspects eyelids, conjunctiva, and ocular surface for visible abnormalities.

examinations.name: External Genital Examination
examinations.description: Inspects external genitalia for lesions, inflammation, or structural abnormalities.

examinations.name: Eyelid Examination
examinations.description: Assesses eyelid position, movement, and lesions affecting ocular protection.

examinations.name: Femoral Nerve Stretch Test
examinations.description: Stretches femoral nerve to assess L2-L4 radiculopathy or neuropathy.

examinations.name: Ferriman-Gallway Score
examinations.description: Scores hair growth pattern to quantify hirsutism severity.

examinations.name: Fetal Heart Rate Auscultation
examinations.description: Listens to fetal heart rate to assess fetal wellbeing.

examinations.name: Finger-to-Floor Distance Test
examinations.description: Measures trunk flexion by distance from fingertips to floor.

examinations.name: Gonioscopy
examinations.description: Examines anterior chamber angle to assess glaucoma and angle anatomy.

examinations.name: Hip Abduction Test
examinations.description: Assesses hip range of motion and pain during abduction.

examinations.name: Nasopharyngeal Examination
examinations.description: Evaluates nasopharynx for masses, inflammation, or obstruction.

examinations.name: Neurological Examination
examinations.description: Assesses mental status, cranial nerves, strength, sensation, reflexes, and coordination.

examinations.name: Occiput-to-Wall Test
examinations.description: Measures thoracic kyphosis by gap between occiput and wall.

examinations.name: Ocular Motility Examination
examinations.description: Assesses extraocular movements to detect palsies or restrictions.

examinations.name: Ophthalmoscopy
examinations.description: Examines retina and optic disc using an ophthalmoscope.

examinations.name: Oral Examination
examinations.description: Inspects lips, mucosa, teeth, gums, tongue, and palate for abnormalities.

examinations.name: Oropharyngeal Examination
examinations.description: Inspects tonsils, uvula, and posterior pharynx for inflammation or lesions.

examinations.name: Orthostatic Vital Signs
examinations.description: Measures vitals lying and standing to assess orthostatic hypotension.

examinations.name: Otoscopy
examinations.description: Examines ear canal and tympanic membrane with an otoscope.

examinations.name: Patellar Grinding Test (Clarke's Sign)
examinations.description: Assesses patellofemoral pain by compressing patella during quadriceps contraction.

examinations.name: Patellar Tap Test (Ballottement)
examinations.description: Detects knee effusion by tapping patella for a floating sensation.

examinations.name: Patrick's Test (FABER)
examinations.description: Assesses hip or sacroiliac pathology with flexion-abduction-external rotation.

examinations.name: Pelvic Compression and Distraction Test
examinations.description: Provokes sacroiliac pain by compressing or distracting the pelvic ring.

examinations.name: Pelvic Examination
examinations.description: Evaluates female pelvic organs via inspection and bimanual palpation.

examinations.name: Penile Measurement
examinations.description: Measures penile length and girth for clinical assessment or documentation.

examinations.name: Periodontal Probing
examinations.description: Measures periodontal pocket depth to assess gum disease severity.

examinations.name: Phalen's Maneuver
examinations.description: Flexes wrists to reproduce median nerve symptoms of carpal tunnel syndrome.

examinations.name: Physical Examination
examinations.description: Systematic assessment using inspection, palpation, percussion, and auscultation.

examinations.name: Piedallu's Sign
examinations.description: Assesses sacroiliac dysfunction via asymmetry of posterior superior iliac spine movement.

examinations.name: Pneumatic Otoscopy
examinations.description: Assesses eardrum mobility by applying air pressure with an otoscope bulb.

examinations.name: Probing Examination
examinations.description: Explores depth and patency of wounds, tracts, or sinus openings with a probe.

examinations.name: Prone Knee Flexion Test
examinations.description: Assesses femoral nerve or lumbar root irritation with prone knee flexion.

examinations.name: Pull Test
examinations.description: Assesses postural stability by observing balance response to a backward pull.

examinations.name: Pupillary Light Reflex Test
examinations.description: Assesses pupillary constriction and symmetry in response to light.

examinations.name: Red Reflex Test
examinations.description: Screens for ocular media opacities by observing retinal red reflection.

examinations.name: Schober's Test
examinations.description: Measures lumbar spine flexion to assess for reduced mobility.

examinations.name: Single-leg Squat Test
examinations.description: Evaluates lower-limb strength, balance, and dynamic knee control.

examinations.name: Skin Examination
examinations.description: Inspects skin for lesions, rashes, color changes, and abnormalities.

examinations.name: Skin and Sclera Examination
examinations.description: Checks skin and sclera for jaundice, pallor, and discoloration.

examinations.name: Slit Lamp Examination
examinations.description: Microscopic examination of anterior eye structures using a slit beam.

examinations.name: Slit Lamp Examination with Fluorescein Staining
examinations.description: Uses fluorescein to detect corneal defects and assess tear film.

examinations.name: Spinal Lateral Flexion Test
examinations.description: Assesses side-bending range of motion of the spine.

examinations.name: Spurling Test
examinations.description: Provokes cervical radiculopathy symptoms by neck extension and compression.

examinations.name: Strabismus Evaluation
examinations.description: Assesses eye alignment and extraocular movements for ocular misalignment.

examinations.name: Strabismus Evaluation (Hirschberg Test)
examinations.description: Estimates ocular alignment by corneal light reflex position.

examinations.name: Straight Leg Raise Test (SLRT)
examinations.description: Assesses sciatic nerve irritation by raising the extended leg.

examinations.name: Talar Tilt Test
examinations.description: Assesses lateral ankle ligament instability by tilting the talus.

examinations.name: Thomas Test
examinations.description: Assesses hip flexor tightness and hip extension range of motion.

examinations.name: Tinel's Sign
examinations.description: Elicits tingling by tapping a nerve to assess nerve irritation.

examinations.name: Tonometry
examinations.description: Measures intraocular pressure to screen for glaucoma.

examinations.name: Vital Signs
examinations.description: Measures temperature, pulse, respiration, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation.

examinations.name: Wood's Lamp Examination
examinations.description: Uses ultraviolet light to detect fungal infection or pigment changes.

examinations.name: von Graefe's Sign
examinations.description: Assesses lid lag on downward gaze, often seen in hyperthyroidism.

examinations.name: Adrenal Function Tests
examinations.description: Evaluates adrenal hormone production and reserve for dysfunction.

examinations.name: Adrenocorticotropic Hormone (ACTH) Test
examinations.description: Measures ACTH to assess pituitary-adrenal axis function.

examinations.name: Alpha-Fetoprotein (AFP) Test
examinations.description: Measures AFP to screen for liver cancer or monitor tumors.

examinations.name: Anemia Profile
examinations.description: Assesses anemia with CBC, iron studies, and related markers.

examinations.name: Anti-Müllerian Hormone (AMH) Test
examinations.description: Measures AMH to estimate ovarian reserve and fertility potential.

examinations.name: Arterial Blood Gas (ABG)
examinations.description: Measures arterial oxygenation, ventilation, and acid-base status.

examinations.name: B-type Natriuretic Peptide (BNP)
examinations.description: Measures BNP to help assess heart failure severity.

examinations.name: Beta-2 Microglobulin Test
examinations.description: Measures beta-2 microglobulin to assess lymphoproliferative disease and kidney function.

examinations.name: Blood Ammonia Test
examinations.description: Measures ammonia to assess liver function and encephalopathy risk.

examinations.name: Blood Biomarker Analysis
examinations.description: Analyzes specific blood markers to assess disease activity or risk.

examinations.name: Blood Glucose Test
examinations.description: Measures blood sugar to screen for or monitor diabetes.

examinations.name: Blood Tests
examinations.description: Laboratory analysis of blood to assess health and diagnose disease.

examinations.name: Blood Typing and Cross-matching
examinations.description: Determines blood type and compatibility for transfusion.

examinations.name: Bone Marrow Examination
examinations.description: Evaluates marrow cells to diagnose hematologic disorders.

examinations.name: Bone Turnover Markers (BTMs)
examinations.description: Measures bone formation and resorption markers to assess bone metabolism.

examinations.name: C-Reactive Protein (CRP)
examinations.description: Measures CRP as a marker of inflammation or infection.

examinations.name: Calcitonin Test
examinations.description: Measures calcitonin to evaluate medullary thyroid cancer risk.

examinations.name: Cardiac Enzyme Panel
examinations.description: Measures cardiac biomarkers to detect myocardial injury.

examinations.name: Catecholamine Assay
examinations.description: Measures catecholamines to evaluate pheochromocytoma or sympathetic overactivity.

examinations.name: Chromogranin A (CgA) Test
examinations.description: Measures CgA as a marker for neuroendocrine tumors.

examinations.name: Chromogranin B (CgB) Test
examinations.description: Measures CgB to help evaluate neuroendocrine tumor activity.

examinations.name: Coagulation Profile
examinations.description: Assesses clotting function using tests like PT, INR, and aPTT.

examinations.name: Complete Blood Count (CBC)
examinations.description: Measures blood cell counts to assess anemia, infection, and platelets.

examinations.name: Comprehensive Metabolic Panel (CMP)
examinations.description: Assesses electrolytes, kidney function, liver enzymes, and glucose.

examinations.name: Cortisol Test
examinations.description: Measures cortisol to assess adrenal function and stress response.

examinations.name: Erythrocyte Enzyme Assay
examinations.description: Measures red cell enzyme activity to diagnose enzymopathies.

examinations.name: Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate (ESR)
examinations.description: Measures ESR as a nonspecific marker of inflammation.

examinations.name: Fasting Blood Glucose (FBG)
examinations.description: Measures fasting glucose to screen for diabetes or prediabetes.

examinations.name: Gastric Cancer Screening Panel
examinations.description: Blood markers assessing gastric mucosal status and cancer risk.

examinations.name: Hemoglobin A1c (HbA1c)
examinations.description: Measures average blood glucose over the past 2-3 months.

examinations.name: Hemoglobin Electrophoresis
examinations.description: Separates hemoglobin types to detect sickle cell and thalassemias.

examinations.name: High-sensitivity C-reactive Protein (hs-CRP)
examinations.description: Measures low-level inflammation to help assess cardiovascular risk.

examinations.name: Homocysteine Test
examinations.description: Measures homocysteine to assess B-vitamin status and vascular risk.

examinations.name: Hormone Panel
examinations.description: Measures multiple hormones to evaluate endocrine function and imbalances.

examinations.name: Human Chorionic Gonadotropin (hCG) Test
examinations.description: Detects hCG to confirm pregnancy or monitor related conditions.

examinations.name: Inflammatory Marker Panel
examinations.description: Checks multiple blood markers to assess systemic inflammation or infection.

examinations.name: Insulin and C-peptide Release Test
examinations.description: Assesses insulin secretion by measuring insulin and C-peptide responses.

examinations.name: Iron Studies
examinations.description: Evaluates iron status using ferritin, iron, TIBC, and saturation.

examinations.name: Lipid Panel
examinations.description: Measures cholesterol and triglycerides to assess cardiovascular risk.

examinations.name: Liver Fibrosis Panel
examinations.description: Estimates liver scarring risk using blood biomarkers and algorithms.

examinations.name: Liver Function Tests (LFTs)
examinations.description: Measures liver enzymes and proteins to assess liver injury and function.

examinations.name: Liver and Renal Function Tests (LFTs/RFTs)
examinations.description: Assesses liver and kidney function with combined blood chemistry tests.

examinations.name: Maternal Serum Screening
examinations.description: Screens pregnancy blood markers for fetal chromosomal or neural tube risks.

examinations.name: Metabolic Screening
examinations.description: Screens blood and urine for metabolic disorders and nutrient abnormalities.

examinations.name: Metanephrines Test
examinations.description: Measures metanephrines to evaluate for pheochromocytoma or paraganglioma.

examinations.name: N-terminal Pro-B-type Natriuretic Peptide (NT-proBNP)
examinations.description: Measures NT-proBNP to help diagnose and monitor heart failure.

examinations.name: Oral Glucose Tolerance Test (OGTT)
examinations.description: Measures blood glucose after oral sugar to diagnose diabetes or gestational diabetes.

examinations.name: Pancreatic Enzyme Test
examinations.description: Measures amylase and lipase to evaluate pancreatitis or pancreatic injury.

examinations.name: Parathyroid Hormone (PTH) Test
examinations.description: Measures PTH to evaluate calcium balance and parathyroid function.

examinations.name: Peripheral Blood Smear
examinations.description: Microscopic review of blood cells to assess morphology and abnormalities.

examinations.name: Pituitary Hormone Panel
examinations.description: Measures pituitary hormones to evaluate pituitary function and regulation.

examinations.name: Plasma Amino Acid Analysis
examinations.description: Measures plasma amino acids to diagnose metabolic or nutritional disorders.

examinations.name: Plasma Arginine Vasopressin (AVP) Test
examinations.description: Measures AVP to evaluate water balance disorders and diabetes insipidus.

examinations.name: Procalcitonin (PCT)
examinations.description: Measures procalcitonin to help detect bacterial infection and sepsis severity.

examinations.name: Renal Function Tests (RFTs)
examinations.description: Assesses kidney function using creatinine, urea, and related measures.

examinations.name: Renin-Angiotensin-Aldosterone System (RAAS) Panel
examinations.description: Measures renin and aldosterone to evaluate hypertension and adrenal disorders.

examinations.name: Serum Angiotensin-Converting Enzyme (SACE) Assay
examinations.description: Measures ACE level to support evaluation of sarcoidosis and related disorders.

examinations.name: Serum Electrolytes
examinations.description: Measures sodium, potassium, chloride, and bicarbonate for fluid-electrolyte balance.

examinations.name: Serum Enzyme Analysis
examinations.description: Measures specific enzymes to assess tissue damage or organ function.

examinations.name: Serum Erythropoietin Level
examinations.description: Measures erythropoietin to evaluate anemia or polycythemia causes.

examinations.name: Serum Protein Electrophoresis (SPEP)
examinations.description: Separates serum proteins to detect monoclonal gammopathies.

examinations.name: Serum Serotonin (5-HT) Test
examinations.description: Measures serotonin to evaluate for carcinoid syndrome or related disorders.

examinations.name: Sex Hormone Panel
examinations.description: Measures sex hormones to assess fertility, puberty, or endocrine disorders.

examinations.name: Thrombopoietin (TPO) Test
examinations.description: Measures thrombopoietin to evaluate causes of low platelet counts.

examinations.name: Thyroid Function Tests (TFTs)
examinations.description: Measures thyroid hormones and TSH to assess thyroid function.

examinations.name: Trace Element Testing
examinations.description: Measures trace minerals to detect deficiencies or toxic exposures.

examinations.name: Tumor Marker Test
examinations.description: Measures cancer-associated proteins to aid monitoring or risk assessment.

examinations.name: Vitamin D Test
examinations.description: Measures vitamin D level to assess bone and mineral health.

examinations.name: Vitamin and Mineral Assay
examinations.description: Measures selected vitamins and minerals to assess nutritional status.

examinations.name: 24-Hour Urinary Thiamine Test
examinations.description: Measures thiamine excretion over 24 hours to assess vitamin B1 status.

examinations.name: 24-Hour Urine Electrolytes
examinations.description: Measures urine electrolytes over 24 hours to assess renal handling.

examinations.name: 24-Hour Urine Metabolic Evaluation
examinations.description: Assesses urine metabolites to evaluate kidney stone risk and metabolism.

examinations.name: 24-Hour Urine Protein Test
examinations.description: Measures total protein excretion to assess kidney disease.

examinations.name: Fecal Fluoride Test
examinations.description: Measures fluoride in stool to assess fluoride exposure or intake.

examinations.name: Fecal Occult Blood Test (FOBT)
examinations.description: Detects hidden blood in stool for colorectal bleeding screening.

examinations.name: Meares-Stamey Four-Glass Test
examinations.description: Cultures sequential urine and prostatic fluid to localize prostatitis infection.

examinations.name: Stool Color Card Examination
examinations.description: Compares stool color to screen infants for cholestasis and biliary atresia.

examinations.name: Stool Ova and Parasites Examination (O&P)
examinations.description: Microscopic exam for parasite eggs and organisms in stool.

examinations.name: Stool Routine Test
examinations.description: Evaluates stool for infection signs, blood, and basic digestive abnormalities.

examinations.name: Two-Glass Test
examinations.description: Compares first and midstream urine to help localize urinary tract infection.

examinations.name: Urinalysis (UA)
examinations.description: Screens urine for infection, kidney disease, and metabolic disorders.

examinations.name: Urinary 5-Hydroxyindoleacetic Acid (5-HIAA) Test
examinations.description: Measures urine 5-HIAA to evaluate serotonin-secreting carcinoid tumors.

examinations.name: Urinary 5-Hydroxytryptophan (5-HTP) Test
examinations.description: Measures urinary 5-HTP, a serotonin precursor, for metabolic evaluation.

examinations.name: Urinary Calcium Test
examinations.description: Measures urine calcium to assess stone risk and calcium metabolism.

examinations.name: Urinary Cystine Test
examinations.description: Detects elevated urinary cystine to evaluate cystinuria and stone risk.

examinations.name: Urinary Electrolyte Panel
examinations.description: Measures urine electrolytes to assess renal handling and fluid balance.

examinations.name: Urinary Fluoride Test
examinations.description: Measures urine fluoride to assess fluoride exposure or toxicity.

examinations.name: Urinary Hydroxyproline Test
examinations.description: Measures urinary hydroxyproline to assess collagen breakdown and bone turnover.

examinations.name: Urinary Lysozyme Test
examinations.description: Measures urinary lysozyme to assess tubular injury or monocytic leukemia.

examinations.name: Urinary Organic Acid Analysis
examinations.description: Profiles urinary organic acids to detect inborn metabolic disorders.

examinations.name: Urinary Pterin Analysis
examinations.description: Measures urinary pterins to evaluate neurotransmitter and BH4 metabolism disorders.

examinations.name: Urinary Serotonin (5-HT) Test
examinations.description: Measures urinary serotonin to evaluate serotonin-producing tumors.

examinations.name: Urinary Stone Analysis
examinations.description: Analyzes passed or removed stones to determine composition and cause.

examinations.name: Urinary Succinylacetone Level
examinations.description: Detects urine succinylacetone for diagnosis of tyrosinemia type I.

examinations.name: Urinary Uric Acid Test
examinations.description: Measures urine uric acid to assess gout risk and stone formation.

examinations.name: Urinary Vanillylmandelic Acid (VMA) Test
examinations.description: Measures urine VMA to assess catecholamine-secreting tumors.

examinations.name: Urinary White Blood Cell Excretion Rate Test
examinations.description: Measures urinary WBC excretion to assess urinary tract inflammation.

examinations.name: Urine Culture
examinations.description: Grows bacteria from urine to diagnose urinary tract infection.

examinations.name: Urine Protein Electrophoresis (UPEP)
examinations.description: Separates urine proteins to detect monoclonal proteins and kidney disease.

examinations.name: Urine hCG Pregnancy Test
examinations.description: Detects hCG in urine to confirm pregnancy.

examinations.name: Actinomyces Antibody Test
examinations.description: Detects antibodies suggesting Actinomyces infection or exposure.

examinations.name: Allergen Testing (Bioresonance)
examinations.description: Alternative method claiming to detect allergies; not scientifically validated.

examinations.name: Allergy Testing
examinations.description: Identifies allergen sensitivities using skin testing or specific IgE blood tests.

examinations.name: Anti-Actin Antibody
examinations.description: Detects anti-actin antibodies, supportive of autoimmune hepatitis type 1.

examinations.name: Anti-Cyclic Citrullinated Peptide (Anti-CCP) Antibody
examinations.description: Detects anti-CCP antibodies to help diagnose rheumatoid arthritis.

examinations.name: Anti-Double Stranded DNA (anti-dsDNA) Antibody
examinations.description: Detects anti-dsDNA antibodies to support lupus diagnosis and monitor activity.

examinations.name: Anti-Endomysial Antibody (EMA)
examinations.description: Detects EMA antibodies to support diagnosis of celiac disease.

examinations.name: Anti-Endothelial Cell Antibody (AECA)
examinations.description: Detects antibodies against endothelium, associated with some vasculitic disorders.

examinations.name: Anti-Intrinsic Factor Antibody
examinations.description: Detects antibodies causing B12 malabsorption in pernicious anemia.

examinations.name: Anti-Keratin Antibody (AKA)
examinations.description: Detects anti-keratin antibodies associated with rheumatoid arthritis.

examinations.name: Anti-Mitochondrial Antibody (AMA)
examinations.description: Detects AMA to support diagnosis of primary biliary cholangitis.

examinations.name: Anti-Parietal Cell Antibody
examinations.description: Detects antibodies linked to autoimmune gastritis and pernicious anemia risk.

examinations.name: Anti-Perinuclear Factor (APF)
examinations.description: Detects APF antibodies associated with rheumatoid arthritis.

examinations.name: Anti-Platelet Antibody Test
examinations.description: Detects antibodies against platelets in suspected immune thrombocytopenia.

examinations.name: Anti-Reticulin Antibody
examinations.description: Detects anti-reticulin antibodies, historically associated with celiac disease.

examinations.name: Anti-Smooth Muscle Antibody (ASMA)
examinations.description: Detects ASMA to support autoimmune hepatitis diagnosis.

examinations.name: Antigen Detection Test
examinations.description: Detects specific microbial antigens for rapid infection diagnosis.

examinations.name: Antigen-Antibody Testing
examinations.description: Detects antigens and/or antibodies to diagnose infections or immune status.

examinations.name: Antineutrophil Cytoplasmic Antibodies (ANCA) Profile
examinations.description: Measures ANCA to help diagnose and classify ANCA-associated vasculitis.

examinations.name: Antinuclear Antibody (ANA) Profile
examinations.description: Screens for ANA and patterns to evaluate systemic autoimmune disease.

examinations.name: Antiphospholipid Antibody (APA) Panel
examinations.description: Tests antiphospholipid antibodies to assess clotting risk and APS.

examinations.name: Antistreptolysin O (ASO) Titre
examinations.description: Measures ASO antibodies to assess recent streptococcal infection.

examinations.name: Autoimmune Hepatitis (AIH) Antibody Profile
examinations.description: Measures AIH-related autoantibodies to support autoimmune hepatitis diagnosis.

examinations.name: Autoimmune Screening Panel
examinations.description: Panel of autoantibodies used to evaluate suspected autoimmune disease.

examinations.name: Autologous Serum Skin Test (ASST)
examinations.description: Skin test using patient serum to evaluate autoimmune chronic urticaria.

examinations.name: Bullous Pemphigoid (BP) Antibody Test
examinations.description: Detects BP180/BP230 antibodies to support bullous pemphigoid diagnosis.

examinations.name: C1-esterase Inhibitor Assay
examinations.description: Measures C1 inhibitor to diagnose hereditary or acquired angioedema.

examinations.name: Casoni Intradermal Test
examinations.description: Historical skin test for hydatid disease; largely obsolete.

examinations.name: Chlamydia pneumoniae Antibody Test
examinations.description: Detects antibodies indicating past or recent Chlamydia pneumoniae infection.

examinations.name: Cold Agglutinin Test
examinations.description: Detects cold-reactive antibodies causing hemolysis in cold agglutinin disease.

examinations.name: Complement Component Analysis
examinations.description: Measures complement protein levels to assess immune and inflammatory function.

examinations.name: Complement Fixation Test (CFT)
examinations.description: Detects specific antibodies by measuring complement consumption in a reaction.

examinations.name: Conjunctival Provocation Test
examinations.description: Applies allergen to eye to evaluate immediate allergic conjunctival response.

examinations.name: Cryoglobulin Test
examinations.description: Detects cold-precipitating proteins in blood linked to vasculitis and infection.

examinations.name: Cysticercus Antibody Test
examinations.description: Detects antibodies against Taenia solium larvae causing cysticercosis.

examinations.name: Cytokine Profile
examinations.description: Measures multiple cytokines to evaluate immune activation and inflammation patterns.

examinations.name: Cytomegalovirus (CMV) Antibody Test
examinations.description: Detects CMV antibodies to assess past exposure or recent infection.

examinations.name: Dengue Virus Antibody Test
examinations.description: Detects dengue antibodies to support diagnosis of current or past infection.

examinations.name: Direct Antiglobulin Test (DAT)
examinations.description: Detects antibodies or complement bound to red blood cells.

examinations.name: Direct Immunofluorescence Assay (DFA)
examinations.description: Uses fluorescent antibodies to detect antigens directly in patient specimens.

examinations.name: Dot Immunogold Filtration Assay (DIGFA)
examinations.description: Rapid membrane test using gold labels to detect specific antibodies or antigens.

examinations.name: Echinococcus Antibody Test
examinations.description: Detects antibodies to Echinococcus, supporting diagnosis of hydatid disease.

examinations.name: Enzyme-Linked Immunosorbent Assay (ELISA)
examinations.description: Enzyme-based assay to detect or quantify specific antibodies or antigens.

examinations.name: Epstein-Barr Virus (EBV) Antibody Panel
examinations.description: Measures EBV antibodies to determine acute, past, or reactivated infection.

examinations.name: Filariasis Antibody Test
examinations.description: Detects antibodies to filarial parasites causing lymphatic or tissue filariasis.

examinations.name: Flow Cytometry Immunophenotyping
examinations.description: Identifies cell populations by surface markers using flow cytometry.

examinations.name: Globulin Precipitation Test
examinations.description: Assesses abnormal globulins by observing precipitation reactions in serum.

examinations.name: HLA-B27 Typing
examinations.description: Determines HLA-B27 status associated with spondyloarthritis risk.

examinations.name: HLA-DR4 Typing
examinations.description: Determines HLA-DR4 allele associated with autoimmune disease susceptibility.

examinations.name: Helicobacter Pylori Antibody Test
examinations.description: Detects antibodies to H. pylori indicating exposure or infection.

examinations.name: Hemagglutination Inhibition (HI) Test
examinations.description: Measures antibodies that block viral hemagglutination of red blood cells.

examinations.name: Hepatitis B Virus (HBV) Panel
examinations.description: Measures HBV antigens and antibodies to stage infection and immunity.

examinations.name: Hepatitis C Virus (HCV) Antibody Test

examinations.description: Samples an under-skin nodule to determine its cause.

examinations.name: Synovial Biopsy

examinations.name: Testicular Biopsy

examinations.name: Thinprep Cytologic Test (TCT)

examinations.name: Thyroid Fine Needle Aspiration (Thyroid FNA)

examinations.name: Tongue Biopsy
examinations.description: Samples tongue tissue to diagnose ulcers, dysplasia, or cancer.

examinations.name: Tzanck Smear

examinations.name: Vaginal Cytology
examinations.description: Examines vaginal cells to assess infection, atrophy, or dysplasia.

examinations.name: 16S rRNA Gene Sequencing

examinations.name: C-MYC Gene Analysis
examinations.description: Assesses C-MYC alterations associated with certain cancers.

examinations.name: Chromosomal Microarray Analysis (CMA)
examinations.description: Detects chromosomal copy number changes and regions of homozygosity.

examinations.name: Chromosome Breakage Study
examinations.description: Evaluates chromosomal fragility for inherited DNA repair disorders.

examinations.name: Cytogenetic Analysis
examinations.description: Analyzes chromosomes to detect numerical or structural abnormalities.

examinations.name: DNA Fingerprinting (Profiling)
examinations.description: Compares DNA patterns for identity, relatedness, or sample matching.

examinations.name: Fetal Karyotype Analysis
examinations.description: Analyzes fetal chromosomes to detect aneuploidy and rearrangements.

examinations.name: Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization (FISH)
examinations.description: Uses fluorescent probes to detect specific DNA changes in cells.

examinations.name: Fragile X Syndrome Genetic Testing

examinations.name: Genetic Mutation Analysis (Genetic Sequencing)
examinations.description: Sequences genes to identify pathogenic variants causing disease.

examinations.name: Genetic Testing
examinations.description: Analyzes DNA to identify inherited or acquired disease-related variants.

examinations.name: HLA-DQ2/HLA-DQ8 Genotyping
examinations.description: Tests HLA types associated with celiac disease risk.

examinations.name: Hepatitis C Virus (HCV) Genotyping
examinations.description: Determines HCV genotype to guide antiviral treatment selection.

examinations.name: IGHV Mutation Analysis
examinations.description: Assesses IGHV mutation status for chronic lymphocytic leukemia prognosis.

examinations.name: Karyotype Analysis (Karyotyping)
examinations.description: Visualizes chromosomes to detect large-scale genetic abnormalities.

examinations.name: Molecular Genetic Testing
examinations.description: Detects gene variants using DNA- or RNA-based molecular methods.

examinations.name: Multiplex Ligation-dependent Probe Amplification (MLPA)
examinations.description: Detects exon-level deletions or duplications in specific genes.

examinations.name: Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS)
examinations.description: High-throughput sequencing to detect variants across multiple genes.

examinations.name: Non-Invasive Prenatal Testing (NIPT)
examinations.description: Screens fetal aneuploidy using cell-free DNA from maternal blood.

examinations.name: Nucleic Acid Hybridization
examinations.description: Detects specific DNA or RNA sequences using complementary probes.

examinations.name: Prenatal Genetic Testing
examinations.description: Tests fetal genetic conditions during pregnancy using blood or invasive sampling.

examinations.name: RNA Sequencing (RNA-seq)
examinations.description: Sequences RNA to measure gene expression and detect fusion transcripts.

examinations.name: Repeat-Primed Capillary Electrophoresis PCR (RP-CE PCR)
examinations.description: Detects pathogenic repeat expansions using repeat-primed PCR sizing.

examinations.name: Southern Blot Analysis
examinations.description: Detects specific DNA fragments, including large repeat expansions or rearrangements.

examinations.name: 24-hour Esophageal pH/Impedance Monitoring
examinations.description: Measures reflux episodes over 24 hours using pH and impedance sensors.

examinations.name: 6-Minute Walk Test (6MWT)

examinations.name: ACTH Stimulation Test

examinations.name: Acute Vasoreactivity Test (AVT)

examinations.name: Alcohol Provocation Test

examinations.name: Allergy Skin Test
examinations.description: Detects allergic sensitization via skin prick or intradermal allergens.

examinations.name: Ambulatory Blood Pressure Monitoring (ABPM)

examinations.name: Anorectal Manometry

examinations.name: Audiometry

examinations.name: Autonomic Function Test

examinations.name: Bernstein Test

examinations.name: Body Composition Analysis (BCA)
examinations.description: Estimates fat, muscle, and body water using impedance or other methods.

examinations.name: Breath Test

examinations.name: Cardiac Stress Test

examinations.name: Cardiopulmonary Exercise Testing (CPET)

examinations.name: Colonic Transit Study

examinations.name: Color Vision Test

examinations.name: Contrast Sensitivity Test

examinations.name: Corneal Endothelial Cell Count

examinations.name: Corneal Pachymetry

examinations.name: Electronic Fetal Monitoring (EFM)

examinations.name: Endocrine Provocation Tests

examinations.name: Erectile Function Testing

examinations.name: Esophageal Manometry

examinations.name: Eustachian Tube Function Test

examinations.name: Exercise Stress Test (EST)

examinations.name: Gait Analysis

examinations.name: GnRH Stimulation Test

examinations.name: Hemodynamic Monitoring

examinations.name: Hormonal Challenge Test

examinations.name: Indocyanine Green (ICG) Clearance Test

examinations.name: Intraocular Pressure (IOP) Measurement

examinations.name: Lacrimal Function Test

examinations.name: Metabolic Loading Test

examinations.name: Nasal Provocation Test

examinations.name: Neostigmine Test

examinations.name: Neuropsychological Assessment

examinations.name: Ocular Muscle Function Test

examinations.name: Olfactory Testing

examinations.name: Orthoptic Evaluation

examinations.name: Phototesting

examinations.name: Psychological Assessment

examinations.name: Tinnitus Evaluation

examinations.name: Hair Microscopy

examinations.name: Methylene Blue Test

examinations.name: Neuropsychological Testing

examinations.name: Newborn Screening

examinations.name: Pericardiocentesis

examinations.name: Preoperative Evaluation

examinations.name: Probe Test

examinations.name: Self-Rating Anxiety Scale (SAS)

examinations.name: Skin Allergy Test

examinations.name: Skin Hydration Measurement

examinations.name: Skin Pathergy Test

examinations.name: Thiamine Loading Test

examinations.name: Tuberculosis (TB) Screening

examinations.name: Tympanocentesis

examinations.name: Urea Breath Test (UBT)

examinations.name: Urethral Sounding

examinations.name: Victorian Institute of Sport Assessment-Patellar (VISA-P) Score

examinations.name: Voiding Diary

examinations.name: Adenosine Deaminase (ADA) Test

examinations.name: Ascitic Fluid Analysis

examinations.name: Blood and Urine Organic Acid Analysis

examinations.name: Branched-Chain Alpha-Keto Acid Dehydrogenase (BCKAD) Activity Assay

examinations.name: Bronchoalveolar Lavage Fluid (BALF) Analysis

examinations.name: Calculus Analysis (Stone Analysis)

examinations.name: Carcinoembryonic Antigen (CEA) Test

examinations.name: Cerebrospinal Fluid (CSF) Analysis

examinations.name: Chromogranin C (CgC) Test

examinations.name: Conjunctival Secretion Analysis

examinations.name: Corneal Scraping Analysis

examinations.name: Des-Gamma-Carboxy Prothrombin (DCP) Test

examinations.name: Duodenal Fluid Analysis

examinations.name: Eyelid Secretion Analysis

examinations.name: Gastric Acid Analysis

examinations.name: High-Performance Liquid Chromatography (HPLC)

examinations.name: Human Epididymis Protein 4 (HE4) Test

examinations.name: Laboratory Testing (General)

examinations.name: Lecithin/Sphingomyelin (L/S) Ratio

examinations.name: Magnesium Loading Test

examinations.name: Metabolite Analysis

examinations.name: Nasal Secretion Analysis
examinations.description: Tests nasal discharge for infection, allergy, or inflammatory markers.

examinations.name: Neuron-Specific Enolase (NSE) Test

examinations.name: Pericardial Fluid Analysis

examinations.name: Phosphatidylglycerol (PG) Test

examinations.name: Pleural Fluid Adenosine Deaminase (ADA) Test

examinations.name: Pleural Fluid Analysis

examinations.name: Pregnancy Test (hCG)

examinations.name: Progenitor Cell Culture
examinations.description: Cultures progenitor cells to assess growth, differentiation, or marrow function.

examinations.name: Prostate-Specific Antigen (PSA) Test

examinations.name: Prostatic Fluid Analysis
examinations.description: Examines prostatic secretions for inflammation or infection.

examinations.name: Semen Analysis

examinations.name: Skin Microscopic Examination
examinations.description: Microscopic evaluation of skin scrapings for fungi, mites, or parasites.

examinations.name: Sweat Chloride Test

examinations.name: Synovial Fluid Analysis
examinations.description: Tests joint fluid for crystals, infection, inflammation, or bleeding.

examinations.name: Tissue Serotonin (5-HT) Test

examinations.name: Tumor Marker Panel

examinations.name: Vaginal and Cervical Secretion Analysis

examinations.name: Vaginal pH Test
